QF.id: XIII Introduction to Forwards and Futures_30

question.text: Comparing expiry dates across derivative markets in India: which statement is correct?

answer.text: Equity index futures expire on the last Tuesday (NSE), currency futures expire 2 working days before the last business day at 12:30 PM, and bond futures have specific monthly expiry dates set by the exchange
All derivative contracts across all asset classes expire on the last Thursday of the month
Equity futures expire on the last Friday, currency on the last Wednesday
All contracts expire at 3:30 PM regardless of the asset class

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Each market has distinct expiry rules: Equity index futures on NSE expire on last Tuesday (post-SEBI rationalization). Currency futures expire 2 working days before last business day at 12:30 PM (not at regular close). Bond futures have exchange-specified dates. BSE equity futures expire on last Thursday. **The Trap:** The old convention of all expiring on last Thursday no longer applies after SEBI's derivative rationalization. The 12:30 PM early close for currency is unique. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII for equity, Series I for currency, Series IV for IR — each specifies different expiry rules. This comparison is a key Series XIII topic. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Different expiry dates across markets create specific risk windows — a currency trader may need to manage expiry risk on Tuesday (equity) and a different day (currency) in the same week.
